journal_entry.reason: Production wages are capitalized to work-in-progress inventory as manufacturing costs. Administrative salaries are expensed as period costs. Withholdings create separate liabilities for pension, health insurance, EI, and income tax. Net pay reduces cash.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Mondelez recognized January payroll with two components: production worker wages ($25,000) are manufacturing costs that must be capitalized to work-in-progress inventory under IFRS product costing rules, while administrative salaries ($20,000) are period costs expensed immediately. Total gross payroll is $45,000. Employee-borne statutory deductions totaling $7,750 ($2,000 pension + $3,250 health insurance + $1,050 EI + $1,450 income tax) are withheld and recorded as separate payables to the respective authorities. Net pay of $37,250 ($45,000 - $7,750) was transferred from the chequing account to employees' accounts. The entry records: (1) capitalization of production wages to inventory (Dr WIP), (2) recognition of administrative salary expense (Dr Employee benefits expense), (3) creation of four distinct withholding liabilities (Cr each payable), and (4) cash outflow for net pay (Cr Cash). Total debits = $25,000 + $20,000 = $45,000. Total credits = $2,000 + $3,250 + $1,050 + $1,450 + $37,250 = $45,000. Entry balances.
